message:
policy: bind elevation tokens to the request that claimed them

Elevation approval tokens declare `original_provenance_id` (CDDL
required, FCP_CDDL_V2.cddl:165) specifically to anchor the elevation
to a single provenance. The engine was ignoring that field: a valid
elevation token for request A in zone Z could be replayed against
request B in zone Z for the same operation_id, silently raising B's
integrity label. [HIGH]

`apply_elevation` now walks the approval list per-token (mirroring
`find_execution_approval`), tracks the "saw elevation scope but
criteria did not match" case, and surfaces it as the new
`ApprovalElevationScopeMismatch` reason code. The binding check
requires `scope.original_provenance_id == input.request_object_id`.

Adds three engine-level regression tests covering:
  - token bound to a different request → Deny/ElevationScopeMismatch
  - token bound to this request → Allow
  - no elevation-scoped token at all → Deny/MissingElevation
(the refactor must not collapse missing vs mismatch)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
